title: Terak's Keep

{{Quote|Resigned to their fate, the marauders built a large ancient-style castle, which comforted them with its imposing stone walls.|[[Pfilbee Jhorn]]|<ref name="Illustrated" />}}
'''Terak's Keep''', also known as the '''Marauders' Stronghold''', was a [[Castle/Legends|castle]] built by [[Warlord/Legends|King]] [[Terak]] of the [[Sanyassan Marauders]] during the first [[Standard century/Legends|century]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. This ancient-style fortress was located on the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]], looming over the [[Dragon's Pelt]] savana from an outcropping the [[Dragon's Spine (mountain chain)|Dragon's Spine]] moutain range. After the defeat of Terak during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the castle remained in use for his surviving goons. The remnants of the Marauders were quickly torn apart by internecine strife, but the Keep eventually found its new master in the person of [[Zakul]], the late Terak's son.

==History==
[[File:Back_side_of_the_Keep.png|thumb|left|The back side of the Keep.]]
When the [[Sanyassan Marauders]] crash-landed on the moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] [[100 BBY/Legends|100 BBY]], they subdued some areas of the Endorian plains. In order to organize themselves, they built this enormous, gloomy, and untidy [[Castle/Legends|castle]]<ref name="Illustrated" /> over the [[Dragon's Pelt]] savanna, at the base of the moutain chain called the [[Dragon's Spine (mountain chain)|Dragon's Spine]].<ref name="GatORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> It would serve as barracks and home to their chiefs, the self-proclaimed [[King/Legends|King]] [[Terak]] and the [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-[[Witch/Legends|witch]] [[Charal]].<ref name="CSWE" /> The square-walled castle was built with stone quarried from mines in the Dragon's Spine's foothills.<ref name="GatORW" />

Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|destruction]] of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], the Stronghold was partly damaged during a [[Battle of Endor (Ewok–Sanyassan conflict)|battle between the Sanyassans and the Ewoks]].<ref name="BFE">''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''</ref> After King Terak's untimely [[Death/Legends|death]], the castle was later used as a shelter for remnants of the Sanyassan Marauders. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Yavid]] later tried to become the king, but the order didn't last long.<ref name="db">{{DB|character|generalyavid|Yavid, General}}</ref> All the Sanyassans turned on each other until [[Zakul]], one of Terak's own sons, became the new King of the Marauders.<ref name="GatORW" />

==Layout==
The fortress was made of quarried stone blocks hauled by [[Blurrg/Legends|blurrgs]], the beast of burden of the Marauders. The castle looked squarish and clumsy, with no finesse—but it was a secure and very sturdy place. The front approach of the castle was guarded by carved and horned [[Magma/Legends|lava]]-rock [[Marauders' obelisk|obelisks]] that to warned off enemies and symbolized the fearlessness of the Marauders. Across the castle entranceway, stones were covered with crude Sanyassan hieroglyphs.<ref name="Illustrated" /> The central tower was designed to look one of the obelisks, a stylized square head with a grinning face and a pair of horns.<ref name="RWC">''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]''</ref>

[[File:Keep_moat.png|thumb|The moat's disintegrator shield in action.]]
The stronghold was surrounded by a deep moat filled with murky [[Water/Legends|water]], which the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Noa Briqualon]] described as "as [[Color/Legends|black]] as ink".<ref name="BFE" /> During the construction of the castle, one of the pirates suggested installing the still functioning automatic defense mechanism from their wrecked [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for further protection against assault. A selective disintegrator field shaped like a [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedo]] was installed in the moat. It would target any object that touched the surface of the water.<ref name="Illustrated" />

The fortress contained many dungeons, a throneroom, and was poorly equipped with primitive technology such as torches and [[Candle/Legends|candles]] lightning. The Marauders had canibalized parts of their crashed starcraft as furniture. For his throne, the King Terak had torn the [[Captain/Legends|captain's]] chair from the bridge of the ship and placed it in his audience chamber.<ref name="Illustrated" />

==Behind the scenes==
King Terak's stronghold was first seen in the [[1985]] made-for-TV film ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'', where it appeared as a primitive fortress made of tan stone.<ref name="BFE" /> As of [[2018]], a matte painting of the structure was on display at [[Lucasfilm]]'s headquarters.<ref name="StarWarsShow-Why An Old Door Hangs on the Wall at Lucasfilm, Star Wars Pinball: The Last Jedi Revealed, and More!">{{StarWarsShow|uk2S6QdUogs|Why An Old Door Hangs on the Wall at Lucasfilm, Star Wars Pinball: The Last Jedi Revealed, and More!|sw_url=video/why-an-old-door-hangs-on-the-wall-at-lucasfilm-hq-star-wars-pinball-the-last-jedi-revealed-and-more}}</ref>

In the [[Wikipedia:MMORPG|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', the location in which players may do quests for the Sanyassan Marauders is called the [[Marauder's Stronghold]]. In the game, the Marauder Stronghold looks much different than its depiction in the movie. It is possible that this was simply a limitation in game mechanics, or they are two entirely different locations.<ref name="gal">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>
